overall.description: A first-person view from a ship's bridge at night in a maritime simulator game. The vessel navigates through a harbor using radar and chart displays, passing occasional dock structures while moving steadily forward through calm waters.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge with navigation monitors overlooking bow

events.description: passing vertical dock post on port side
navigating between dock structures with posts visible on both port and starboard sides

agents.ego_description: operating the ship from the bridge, monitoring radar and navigation charts while moving forward through the harbor

entered.detail: three-screen navigation setup with chart plotter, radar, and depth sounder displays
bow of the vessel with anchor equipment and deck lighting visible ahead
metal safety railing along the port side of the bridge wing
metal safety railing along the starboard side of the bridge wing
dark water surface illuminated by navigation lights
vertical dock posts and fenders appearing on the port side as the vessel progresses
vertical dock posts visible on the starboard side when passing through narrow sections

segments.scene_description: From the ship's bridge, the camera moves steadily forward through a harbor at night. Navigation monitors display radar and chart information overhead while the bow cuts through dark water. The vessel passes vertical dock posts on the port side around 4 seconds, then navigates between structures on both sides at 8 seconds before continuing into open water.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous forward movement from a ship's bridge as it navigates from a confined harbor area into the open sea at night. It begins with the vessel cutting through dark water, guided by the glow of radar and chart monitors overhead. As the ship advances, the camera passes a series of vertical dock posts on the port side before threading between industrial structures on both sides. The scene then opens up as the vessel clears the harbor boundaries, finally gliding steadily into the expanse of open water.
